questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The standard reduction potential data at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is given below.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Fe}^{3+} . \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\right)=+0.77 \mathrm{~V} ; \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+} . \mathrm{Fe}\right)=-0.44 \mathrm{~V} ; \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Cu}^{2+} . \mathrm{Cu}\right)=+0.34 \mathrm{~V} ; \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Cu}^{+} . \mathrm{Cu}\right)=+0.52 \mathrm{~V} ; \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)=+1.23 \mathrm{~V} ; \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 4 \mathrm{OH}^{-}\right)=+0.40 \mathrm{~V} \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Cr}^{3+} . \mathrm{Cr}\right)=-0.74 \mathrm{~V} ; \\
& \left.\mathrm{E}^{\circ}\left(\mathrm{Cr}^{2+} . \mathrm{Cr}\right)=-0.91 \mathrm{~V}\right)
\end{aligned}$

Which of the following statements is/are correct on the basis of above data:
options: $\mathrm{O}_2$ is a better oxidising agent in acidic medium.
$\mathrm{Cr}^{2+}$ show disproportionation to Cr and $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$ in water.
$\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$oxidises Fe to $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ to $\mathrm{O}_2$ in acidic medium.
questions.solution:
(A) As $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{O}_2 / \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} ; \mathrm{H}^{+}}^{\circ}\gt\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{O}_2 / \mathrm{OH}^{-}}^{\circ}$
So, $\mathrm{O}_2$ is better oxidising agent in acidic medium.
(B) $3 \mathrm{Cr}^{2+} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}+\mathrm{Cr}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{x} \times 1+2(-0.91)=-0.74 \times 3 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=-2.22+1.82=-0.40 \mathrm{~V} \\
& \quad \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{cell}}^{\circ}=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cr}^{2+} / \mathrm{Cr}}^{\circ}-\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cr}^{3+} / \mathrm{Cr}^{2+}}^{\circ} \\
&=-0.91-(-0.40) \\
&=-0.51 \mathrm{~V}
\end{aligned}$
As this is negative,
So reaction is non spontaneous.
(C) As SRP of $\mathrm{O}_2$ in acidic medium is more than SRP of $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ So, $\mathrm{O}_2$ oxidises $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ to $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$.
(D) As SRP of $\mathrm{Cu}^{+} / \mathrm{Cu}$ is more than SRP of $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+} / \mathrm{Fe}, \mathrm{Cu}^{+}$oxidises Fe .
But SRP of $\mathrm{Cu}^{+} / \mathrm{Cu}$ is less than SRP of $\mathrm{O}_2 / \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ in acidic medium. So $\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$do not oxidise $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ to $\mathrm{O}_2$ in acidic medium. This does not take place.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
An electrochemical cell is formed by the combination of Zn and Ag . For this cell $\Delta H=-n F E_{\text {cell }}$ Here $\Delta \mathrm{H}=$ Enthalpy change $\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}=e . \mathrm{m} . \mathrm{f}$ of the cell that is 1.1 V
Select the correct statement(s) about this cell
options: Entropy change of the cell reaction is zero
Temperature coefficient of the cell is zero
$\Delta \mathrm{G}$ is negative for the cell reaction
Entropy change of the cell reaction is non-zero
questions.solution:
$\Delta \mathrm{S}=\mathrm{nF}\left(\frac{\partial \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{cell}}}{\partial \mathrm{~T}}\right)_{\mathrm{P}}$
$\Delta \mathrm{G}=-\mathrm{nFE}_{\text {cell }}=\Delta \mathrm{H}-\mathrm{T} \Delta \mathrm{~S}$

If $\Delta H=-n F E_{\text {cell }}$, it means
$\Delta S=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ evolve at anode during electrolysis of fused $\mathrm{MgCl}_2$ which produces 13 g Mg . (Atomic mass of $\mathrm{Mg}=24.3$ ) is
questions.solution: (No. of gm equivalent of Mg at cathode $)=\left(\right.$ No of gm equivalent of $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ at Anode $)$ $\frac{13}{24.3 / 2}=\frac{\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}}{35.5}$ $\mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}=37.98 \mathrm{gm}$ or $\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}=0.535$ $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{Cl}_2}=11.98$ lit at NTP
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In a cell, $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})\left|\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}\right|\left|\mathrm{H}^{+}\right| \mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{pt})$
the addition of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ to the cathode compartment will
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_2$    ....(1)
$\mathrm{E}=\mathrm{E}_{\text {cell }}^0-\frac{0.059}{n} \log \frac{\left[\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}_2\right]}{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^2}$     .....(2)
Equilibrium (1) will shift in forward direction and cell potential E will increase.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following solutions
Solution P : aq. solution of $\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2$
Solution Q : aq. solution of $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$
Solution R : conc. aq. solution of NaCl
Solution S : aq. solution of $\mathrm{KNO}_3$
If electrolysis of these solutions takes place using inert electrodes, in different containers. Among the following, set of the solution(s) in which pH does not increase due to electrolysis is/are (assume no volume change due to electrolysis)
questions.solution:
Hint : If more $\mathrm{H}^{+}$is formed pH decreases, but if more $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$is formed pH increases.
Solution :
Electrolysis
(P) Cathode
$\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{~s})$

Anode
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
$\mathrm{pH} \downarrow$

(Q) Cathode
$\mathrm{Ag}^{+}+1 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{~s})$

Anode
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
$\mathrm{pH} \downarrow$

(R) Cathode
$2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}$

Anode
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Cl}^{-} \longrightarrow \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{Cl}_2+1 \mathrm{e}^{-} \\
& \mathrm{pH} \uparrow
\end{aligned}$

(S) Cathode
$2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-}$

Anode
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_2+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The fraction of current carried by each ion in solution is equal to the ratio of conductivity of the ion in solution to the total conductivity of solution. If in a solution which is 0.1 M in $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ and $0.01 \mathrm{M}^2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ (assume complete ionisation). [Given $\lambda_{\mathrm{m}}$ of $\mathrm{Na}^{+}, \mathrm{H}^{+}$and $\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}$ are $50.1 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, 349.8 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $160 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2$ $\mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ respectively]. Select the correct statement.
options: The fraction of total current carried by $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$is more than that of $\mathrm{H}^{+}$
The fraction of total current carried by $\mathrm{H}^{+}$is nore than that of $\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}$
The fraction of total current carried by $\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}$ is more than that of $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$
The fraction of total current carried by each ion is in order $\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}\gt\mathrm{Na}^{+}\gt\mathrm{H}^{+}$
questions.solution:
Conductivity due to $\mathrm{Na}^{+}=$
$\frac{2 \times 0.1 \times 50.1}{1000}=0.010 \mathrm{~s} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$

Conductivity due to $\mathrm{H}^{+}=$
$\frac{2 \times 0.01 \times 349.8}{1000}=0.0069 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$

Conductivity due to $\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}=$
$\frac{0.11 \times 160}{1000}=0.0176 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$

Total conductivity $=0.0345 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$
Fraction of current carried by each ion
$\mathrm{Na}^{+}=\frac{0.01}{0.0345} \simeq 0.29$
$\mathrm{H}^{+}=\frac{0.0069}{0.0345} \simeq 0.20$
$\mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}=\frac{0.0176}{0.0345} \simeq 0.51$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The conductivity of V ml 0.1 M NaOH solution is $0.022 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$. When this solution was neutralised with an equal volume $(\mathrm{V} \mathrm{ml})$ of 0.1 M HCl , the conductivity decreases to $0.0056 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$. A further V ml of the same HCl was added and the conductivity increased to ' X ' $\mathrm{S} \mathrm{cm}^{-1}$. The value of $\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}$ of HCl is $400 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2$ $\mathrm{mol}^{-1}$. Assume all measurements to be done at the same temperature. Then based on this information, select the correct statement.
options: The value of $\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}$ of NaOH is $220 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
The value of $\wedge_{\mathrm{m}}$ of NaCl is $56 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following information
$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Electrolyte} & \begin{array}{l} \text{Molar conductivity at} \\
\text{infinite dilution at} \\
25^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \text{ in } \mathrm{S} \mathrm{cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\end{array} \\
\hline \mathrm{HCl} & 426 \\
\hline \mathrm{NaCl} & 126 \\
\hline \mathrm{NaC}_4 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{O}_2 & 83 \\
\hline\end{array}$
If conductivity of $0.001 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$ solution is $3.83 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$ and neglect the variation of conductivity with concentration. The correct statement(s) about $\mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$ assuming it to be a weak acid is/are
options: The degree of dissociation of acid $\mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$ is $10 \%$
The degree of dissociation of acid $\mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$ is $26 \%$
The dissociation constant of acid $\mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$ is $1.11 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{M}$
The dissociation constant of acid $\mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{O}_2$ is $1.11 \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{M}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{E}_{\text {redox chan }}^{\circ}$ change will be negative for non-spontaneous reaction
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{s})+\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq}) \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Ni}^{2+} / \mathrm{Ni}}^{\circ}-\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cu}^{2+} / \mathrm{Cu}}^{\circ}=-0.25-0.34=-0.59 \mathrm{~V}(-\mathrm{ve}, \text { non }- \text { spontaneous }) \\
& \mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{H}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \\
& \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{H}^{+} / \mathrm{H}_2}^{\circ}-\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cu}^{2+} / \mathrm{Cu}}^{\circ}=0-0.34=-0.34 \mathrm{~V}(-\mathrm{ve}, \text { non }- \text { spontaneous })
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If electrolysis of $1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NiSO}_4$ is carried with inert electrodes at $\mathrm{pH}=7$ at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ (the pressure of gases are taken 1 bar).
$\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Ni}^{2+} / \mathrm{Ni}}^{\circ}=-0.25 \mathrm{~V} \quad \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} / \mathrm{H}_2}^{\circ}=-0.83 \mathrm{~V}$
The product of electrolysis at cathode and at anode (initially) are:
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{H}_2 \quad \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.0 \mathrm{~V}$
$\mathrm{E}=\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{H}^{-} / \mathrm{H}_2}=-\frac{0.059}{1} \log \frac{1}{\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]}$
$=-0.059 \times 7=-0.413$
So, $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{H}^{+} / \mathrm{H}} \lt \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{NN}^2 / / \mathrm{NI}}$
Ni will deposit at cathode
At anode cell reaction
$4 \mathrm{OH}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_2+4 \mathrm{e}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

options: The molar conductivity of KOOC.COONa at infinite dilution is $271.8 \mathrm{Scm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$

The equivalent conductivity of KOOC.COONa at infinite dilution is $197.7 \mathrm{Scm}^2 \mathrm{eq}^{-1}$
